data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: TSLA, CVNA, UPST
data.description:
Among the recent key upgrades and downgrades from Seeking Alpha analysts, Stone Fox Capital questioned whether Tesla would get the most out of its robotaxi efforts. On the positive side, Stephen Ayers upgraded Carvana as financial improvements signal business viability, and The Asian Investor predicted an inflection point for Upstart Holdings’ profitability. 

Upgrades

  • Upstart Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:UPST): Upgrade Sell to Buy by The Asian Investor. The analyst cites the company’s double-digit revenue growth, improving platform metrics, and expected GAAP profitability inflection point in 2025, combined with a significant valuation correction.

    “Upstart submitted a better-than-expected earnings sheet for the first fiscal quarter that showed double-digit top line growth, strong demand for the firm’s AI-supported credit services and considerable improvements in key platform metrics such as automations and EBITDA margins. The massive valuation draw-down paired with an optimistic outlook for FY 2025 (implying full-year GAAP profitability) is a game-changer for me and justifies a change in the stock rating for Upstart.”

  • Carvana Co. (NYSE:CVNA): Upgrade Sell to Hold by Stephen Ayers. The analyst recognizes Carvana’s financial turnaround with strong revenue growth, improved profitability, and positive free cash flow, though remains cautious about its valuation.

    “Carvana’s recent performance merits a rating upgrade from Sell to Hold. It has transitioned to profitability, and the levers are there for more growth and profitability for years to come. While its stock appears overpriced, even with optimistic assumptions, it is not to the extent that it feels ridiculous.”

Downgrades

  • Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA): Bearish rating by Stone Fox Capital. The analyst emphasizes execution risks associated with Tesla’s robotaxi ambitions and expresses concerns about the stock’s current valuation, which already prices in robotaxi success despite limited testing and regulatory preparation.

    “The key investor takeaway is that Tesla already appears valued based on initial robotaxi success, and this doesn’t appear guaranteed. The stock trades at over 180x EPS targets and over 11x sales estimates. After the recent big rally, investors should brace for the normal Tesla disappointment regarding robotaxi services to send the stock lower.”

data.title: Constellation Energy's deal for Calpine clears Texas regulatory review
data.description:
Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG) said Thursday it received regulatory approval from the Public Utility Commission of Texas for its acquisition of Calpine Corporation, which would make Constellation the largest U.S. independent power company.

In January, Constellation (NASDAQ:CEG) announced its agreement to buy privately-held Calpine for $16.4B in one of the biggest-ever U.S. power industry acquisitions.

Consumer groups had protested that the deal would give the merged company too much market power, with the potential to ultimately stifle competition and drive up power bills.

The deal now awaits clearance from the New York State Public Service Commission, the U.S. Federal Energy Regulatory Commission and the U.S. Department of Justice; the company expects the deal will close in Q4 2025.

data.title: Broadcom slips even as Q2 results, guidance top estimates
data.description:
Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) shares fell 2.9% in extended trading on Thursday, even as the semiconductor and software company reported fiscal second-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations.

For the period ending May 4, Broadcom said it earned an adjusted $1.58 per share as revenue rose 20.1% year-over-year to $15B. Semiconductor solutions revenue was $8.41B for the period, while infrastructure revenue was $6.596B.

“Broadcom achieved record second quarter revenue on continued momentum in AI semiconductor solutions and VMware,” said Broadcom CEO and President Hock Tan in a statement. “Q2 AI revenue grew 46% year-over-year to over $4.4 billion, driven by robust demand for AI networking.”

A consensus of analysts expected Broadcom to report adjusted earnings per share of $1.57 on revenue of $14.7B.

Broadcom makes application integrated specific circuits for a number of high profile customers, including Alphabet, Meta, ByteDance and others.

Looking to the third-quarter, Broadcom expects revenue to be about $15.8B, above the $15.75B estimate. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be around 66% of total revenue. 

AI semiconductor revenue in the third-quarter is expected to hit $5.1B, marking 10 consecutive quarters of growth, Tan added.

Broadcom also declared its quarterly dividend of $0.59 per share, payable on June 30, to stockholders of record on June 20. 

The company will host a conference call at 5 p.m. EST to discuss the results.

data.title: Circle stock surges triple-digits after strong public debut
data.description:
Circle Internet Group (NYSE:CRCL) shares scored triple-digit gains in its first day of trading Thursday after the stablecoin issuer raised nearly $1.1B in an upsized initial public offering.

The stock closed out the session up 169% to $83.54 per share, significantly above the $31 IPO price, as well as this week's marketed range of $27-$28 and last week's $24-$26. It officially opened at $69 per share in early afternoon trading.

At one point, CRCL made an intraday high of $103.75, before paring some of the advance. The stock's volatile price action resulted in several trading halts.

CRCL pulled back further in after-hours trading, drifting down 5.2% to $78.89 a share.

Circle (NYSE:CRCL) co-founder Jeremy Allaire, who owns about 18M CRCL shares, in addition to options and restricted stock units, saw his fortune jump to over $1.9B, Bloomberg reported, adding that he sold some additional 1.6M shares in the offering.

Sean Neville, the other co-founder of the company behind stablecoin USD Coin (USDC-USD), owns over $2.8M worth ~$367M, the article said, citing filings.

data.title: Broadcom declares $0.59 dividend
data.description:
• Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) declared $0.59/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 0.91%
  • Payable June 30; for shareholders of record June 20; ex-div June 20.
  • See AVGO Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Superior Industries receives NYSE non-compliance notice
data.description:
• Superior Industries (NYSE:SUP) received a non-compliance notice from the NYSE as its 30-day average market cap was ~$45.9M, below the $50M minimum.
  • As of March 31, 2025, SUP reported a stockholders’ deficit of approximately $288.7M.
  • Superior has 45 days to submit a plan to regain compliance within 18 months, which will be subject to NYSE review.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Pan Global appoints CFO
data.description:
Pan Global Resources (OTCQX:PGZFF) (TSXV:PGZ:CA) announced on Thursday the appointment of Justin Byrd as CFO.

Byrd replaces Andy Marshall, who is set to remain with the company until June 30.

Byrd has served as CFO at Mayfair Gold until July 2024.

data.title: Rubrik Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.15 beats by $0.17, revenue of $278.5M beats by $18.09M
data.description:
• Rubrik press release (NYSE:RBRK): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.15 beats by $0.17.
  • Revenue of $278.5M (+48.7% Y/Y) beats by $18.09M.

  • Rubrik is providing the following guidance for the second quarter of fiscal year 2026 and the full fiscal year 2026:

    • Second Quarter Fiscal 2026 Outlook:
      • Revenue of $281 million to $283 million.
      • Non-GAAP Subscription ARR contribution margin of 4.5% to 5.5%.
      • Non-GAAP EPS of $(0.35) to $(0.33).
      • Weighted-average shares outstanding of approximately 196 million.
    • Full Year 2026 Outlook:
      • Subscription ARR between $1,380 million and $1,388 million.
      • Revenue of $1,179 million to $1,189 million.
      • Non-GAAP Subscription ARR contribution margin of approximately 6.0%.
      • Non-GAAP EPS of $(1.02) to $(0.96).
      • Weighted-average shares outstanding of approximately 198 million.
      • Free cash flow of $65 million to $75 million.\
      •

data.title: Mission Produce Non-GAAP EPS of $0.12 beats by $0.06, revenue of $380.3M beats by $84.15M
data.description:
• Mission Produce press release (NASDAQ:AVO): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.12 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $380.3M (+27.8% Y/Y) beats by $84.15M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $19.1 million, compared to $20.2 million in the same period last year
  • Repurchased $5.2 million of the Company’s common stock during the quarter

  • Net sales in the Marketing & Distribution segment increased $75.4 million or 26% to $362.5 million for the second quarter, driven by increases in avocado pricing as described above.

  •

data.title: Vail Resorts GAAP EPS of $10.54 beats by $0.48, revenue of $1.29B misses by $10M
data.description:
• Vail Resorts press release (NYSE:MTN): Q3 GAAP EPS of $10.54 beats by $0.48.
  • Revenue of $1.29B (+0.8% Y/Y) misses by $10M.
  • As a result of the lower than expected lift ticket visitation during the spring period announced on April 24, 2025, and one-time costs related to the CEO transition announced on May 27, 2025, the Company is updating its guidance for fiscal 2025. The Company now expects net income attributable to Vail Resorts, Inc. to be between $264 million and $298 million, and Resort Reported EBITDA for fiscal 2025 to be between $831 million and $851 million.
